# THAPBI *Phytophthora* ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT)

## About

THAPBI PICT is a sequence based diagnostic/profiling tool from the UK funded
Tree Health and Plant Biosecurity Initiative (THAPBI) [Phyto-Threats project](https://www.forestresearch.gov.uk/research/global-threats-from-phytophthora-spp/),
initially focused on identifying *Phytophthora* species present in Illumina
sequenced environmental samples.

*Phytophthora* (from Greek meaning plant-destroyer) species are economically
important plant pathogens, in both agriculture and forestry. ITS1 is short for
Internal Transcribed Spacer one, which is a region of eukaryotes genomes
between the 18S and 5.8S rRNA genes. This is commonly used for molecular
barcoding, where sequencing this short region can identify species.

With appropriate primer settings and a custom database of full length markers,
THAPBI PICT can be applied to other organisms and/or barcode marker sequences
\- not just *Phytophthora* ITS1. It requires overlapping paired-end Illumina
reads which can be merged to cover the *full* amplicon marker. Longer markers
or fragmented amplicons are not supported. Internally it works by tracking
unique amplicon sequence variants (ASVs), using MD5 checksums as identifiers.

The worked examples include oomycetes, fungi, fish, bats, and plants, and
cover markers in ITS1, ITS2, 12S, 16S, COI, and more. The main criteria has
been mock communities with known species composition.

## Installation

We recommend installing this tool on Linux or macOS using the
[Conda](https://conda.io/) packaging system, via the
[BioConda](https://bioconda.github.io/) channel, which will handle
*all* the dependencies:

```console
$ conda install thapbi-pict
```

Alternatively or on Windows, since [the software is on the Python Package
Index (PyPI)](https://pypi.org/project/thapbi-pict/), the following command
will install it along with its Python dependencies:

```console
$ pip install thapbi-pict
```

However, in this case you will still need to install various external command
line tools. See `INSTALL.rst` for more details (especially for Windows),
and if you want to modify the software read `CONTRIBUTING.rst` as well.

## Quick Start

Once installed, you should be able to run the tool at the command line
using:

```console
$ thapbi_pict
```

This should automatically find the installed copy of the Python code.
Use `thapbi_pict -v` to report the version, or `thapbi_pict -h` for
help.

## Documentation

The [tool documentation](https://thapbi-pict.readthedocs.io/) is hosted by
[Read The Docs](https://readthedocs.org/), generated automatically from the
`docs/` folder.

The documentation includes more detailed discussion of the sample datasets
in the `examples/` folder (which are based on published datasets).

## Citation

If you use THAPBI PICT in your work, please cite our *PeerJ* paper, and give
details of the version and any non-default settings used in your methods:

> Cock *et al.* (2023) "THAPBI PICT - a fast, cautious, and accurate
> metabarcoding analysis pipeline" *PeerJ* **11**:e15648
> <https://doi.org/10.7717/peerj.15648>

You can also cite the software specifically via Zenodo which offers version
specific DOIs as well as <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.4529395> which is for
the latest version.

## Funding

The initial work was supported from 2016 to 2019 under the Tree Health and
Plant Biosecurity Initiative (THAPBI) Phyto-Threats project:

> This research was supported by a grant funded jointly by the
> Biotechnology and Biological Sciences Research Council ([BBSRC](https://bbsrc.ukri.org/)), Department for Environment, Food and Rural
> affairs ([DEFRA](https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/department-for-environment-food-rural-affairs)),
> Economic and Social Research Council ([ESRC](https://esrc.ukri.org)),
> [Forestry Commission](https://www.gov.uk/government/organisations/forestry-commission),
> Natural Environment Research Council ([NERC](https://nerc.ukri.org))
> and [Scottish Government](https://www.gov.scot/), under the Tree
> Health and Plant Biosecurity Initiative, grant number `BB/N023463/1`.

Work from 2020 to 2021 was supported in part under the *Early detection of
Phytophthora in EU and third country nurseries and traded plants (ID-PHYT)*
Euphresco project:

> Funded by DEFRA as part of the Future Proofing Plant Health project in
> support of Euphresco ID-PHYT.

Work from 2022 to May 2024 was partly funded by the Rural & Environment Science
& Analytical Services (RESAS) Division of the Scottish Government.

## Background

THAPBI PICT continues earlier work including:

- <https://github.com/widdowquinn/THAPBI>
- <https://github.com/widdowquinn/THAPBI-pycits>
- <https://github.com/peterthorpe5/THAPBI-pycits>
- <https://github.com/peterthorpe5/public_scripts/tree/master/metapy>
- <https://github.com/peterthorpe5/public_scripts/tree/master/metapy_tools>

## Release History

See the `CHANGELOG.rst` file.

## Development Notes

See file `CONTRIBUTING.rst` for details of the development setup including
Python style conventions, git pre-commit hook, continuous integration and test
coverage, and release process.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.fasta_nr
documentation:
Prepare a non-redundant FASTA file using MD5 naming.

This implements the `thapbi_pict fasta-nr ...` command, using some of the
same code internally as the `thapbi_pict prepare-reads` command.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.fasta_nr.main
documentation: Implement the `thapbi_pict fasta-nr` command.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.denoise
documentation:
Apply UNOISE read-correction to denoise FASTA file(s).

This implements the `thapbi_pict denoise ...` command, which is a simplified
version of the `thapbi_pict sample-tally ...` command intended to be easier
to use outside the THAPBI PICT pipeline.
Copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.denoise.unoise
documentation:
Apply UNOISE2 algorithm.

Argument counts is an (unsorted) dict of sequences (for the same amplicon
marker) as keys, with their total abundance counts as values.

If not specified (i.e. set to zero or None), unoise_alpha defaults to 2.0
and unoise_gamma to 4.

Returns a dict mapping input sequences to centroid sequences, and an empty
dict (no chimera detection performed).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.denoise.usearch
documentation:
Invoke USEARCH to run its implementation of the UNOISE3 algorithm.

Assumes v10 or v11 (or later if the command line API is the same).
Parses the four columns tabbed output.

Returns a dict mapping input sequences to centroid sequences, and a dict
of MD5 checksums of any sequences flagged as chimeras.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.denoise.vsearch
documentation:
Invoke VSEARCH to run its reimplementation of the UNOISE3 algorithm.

Argument counts is an (unsorted) dict of sequences (for the same amplicon
marker) as keys, with their total abundance counts as values.

Returns a dict mapping input sequences to centroid sequences, and a dict
of MD5 checksums of any sequences flagged as chimeras.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.denoise.read_correction
documentation:
Apply builtin UNOISE algorithm or invoke an external tool like VSEARCH.

Argument algorithm is a string, "unoise-l" for our reimplementation of the
UNOISE2 algorithm, or "usearch" or "vsearch" to invoke those tools at the
command line.

Argument counts is an (unsorted) dict of sequences (for the same amplicon
marker) as keys, with their total abundance counts as values.

Returns a dict mapping input sequences to centroid sequences, and dict of
any chimeras detected (empty for some algorithms).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.denoise.main
documentation:
Implement the `thapbi_pict denoise` command.

This is a simplified version of the `thapbi_pict sample-tally` command
which pools one or more FASTA input files before running the UNOISE read
correction algorithm to denoise the dataset. The input sequences should
use the SWARM \<prefix>\_\<abundance> style naming, which is used on output
(taking the first loaded name if a sequence appears more than once).

Arguments min_length and max_length are applied while loading the input
FASTA file(s).

Argument total_min_abundance is applied after read correction.

Results sorted by decreasing abundance, then alphabetically by sequence.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.classify
documentation:
Classifying prepared marker sequences using a marker database.

This implements the `thapbi_pict classify ...` command.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.unique_or_separated
documentation: Return sole element, or a string joining all elements using the separator.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.consoliate_and_sort_taxonomy
documentation:
Remove any redundant entries, returns new sorted list.

Drops zero taxid entries if has matching non-zero entry.

Drops genus only entries if have species level entries.
Note ignoring the TaxID here - would need to know the parent/child
relationship to confirm the genus we're removing does have species
level children in the prediction set.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.taxid_and_sp_lists
documentation:
Return semi-colon separated summary of the taxonomy objects from DB.

Will discard genus level predictions (e.g. 'Phytophthora') if there is a
species level prediction within that genus (e.g. 'Phytophthora infestans').

If there is a single result, returns a tuple of taxid (integer), genus-species,
and debugging comment (strings).

If any of the fields has conflicting values, returns two semi-colon separated
string instead (in the same order so you can match taxid to species, sorting
on the genus-species string).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.perfect_match_in_db
documentation:
Lookup sequence in DB, returns taxid, genus_species, note as tuple.

If the 100% matches in the DB give multiple species, then taxid and
genus_species will be semi-colon separated strings.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.perfect_substr_in_db
documentation:
Lookup sequence in DB, returns taxid, genus_species, note as tuple.

If the matches containing the sequence as a substring give multiple species,
then taxid and genus_species will be semi-colon separated strings.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.apply_method_to_seqs
documentation:
Call given method on each sequence in the dict.

Assumes any abundance filter has already been applied. Input is a dict of
identifiers mapped to upper case sequences.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.method_identity
documentation:
Classify using perfect identity.

This is a deliberately simple approach, in part for testing
purposes. It looks for a perfect identical entry in the database.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.method_substr
documentation:
Classify using perfect identity including as a sub-string.

Like the 'identity' method, but allows for a database where the marker
has not been trimmed, or has been imperfectly trimmed (e.g. primer
mismatch).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_seqs
documentation:
Prepare a set of all the DB marker sequences as upper case strings.

Also setup set of sequences in the DB, and dict of genus to NCBI taxid.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_onebp
documentation: Prepare a set of all the DB marker sequences; set dist to 1.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist2
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 2.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist3
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 3.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist4
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 4.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist5
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 5.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist6
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 6.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist7
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 7.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist8
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 8.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_dist9
documentation: Prepare a set of all DB marker sequences; set dist to 9.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.method_dist
documentation: Classify using edit distance.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.setup_blast
documentation: Prepare a BLAST DB from the marker sequence DB entries.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.method_blast
documentation:
Classify using BLAST.

Another simplistic classifier, run the reads through blastn
against a BLAST database of our marker sequence database entries.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.method_cleanup
documentation:
Free any memory and/or delete any files on disk.

Currently no need to generalise this for the different classifiers, but
could if for example we also needed to delete any files on disk.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.classify.main
documentation:
Implement the `thapbi_pict classify` command.

For use in the pipeline command, returns a filename list of the TSV
classifier output.

The input files should have been prepared with the same or a lower minimum
abundance - this acts as an additional filter useful if exploring the best
threshold.

variables.name: thapbi_pict.classify.MIN_BLAST_COVERAGE
documentation: percentage of query length

variables.name: thapbi_pict.classify.db_seqs
documentation: global dict (seq to genus) for onebp and 1s?g distance classifiers

variables.name: thapbi_pict.classify.max_dist_genus
documentation: global variable for 1s?g distance classifiers

variables.name: thapbi_pict.classify.genus_taxid
documentation: global variable to cache taxids for genus names

modules.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump
documentation:
Code for THAPBI PICT to deal with NCBI taxonomy dumps.

The code is needed initially for loading an NCBI taxdump folder (files
`names.dmp`, `nodes.dmp`, `merged.dmp` etc) into a marker database.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.load_nodes
documentation:
Load the NCBI taxdump nodes.dmp file.

Returns two dicts, the parent/child relationships, and the ranks (values
are lists of taxids).

Default is all ranks, can provide a possibly empty list/set of ranks of
interest.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.load_merged
documentation: Load mapping of merged taxids of interest from NCBI taxdump merged.dmp file.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.load_names
documentation: Load scientific names of species from NCBI taxdump names.dmp file.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.filter_tree
documentation:
Return a filtered version of the tree & ranks dict.

NOTE: Does NOT preserve the original dict order.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.get_ancestor
documentation: Walk up tree until reach a stop node, or root.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.species_or_species_groups
documentation:
Find taxids for species or species groups.

Our "genus" list matches the NCBI rank "genus", and includes child nodes
as aliases (unless they fall on our "species" list or reject list of
"environmental samples" or "unclassified <genus>").

However, our "species" list are either NCBI rank "species" or "species
group" (in the later case child species are taken as aliases).

Does not distinguish between "top level" species, or those under "no rank"
nodes like "environmental samples" or "unclassified Phytophthora" (taxid
211524),

Yields (species taxid, genus taxid) tuples.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.not_top_species
documentation:
Find all 'minor' species, takes set of species taxid to ignore.

Will map assorted sub-species (i.e. any nodes under top_species) to the
parent species, e.g. varietas 'Phytophthora nicotianae var. parasitica'
NCBI:txid4791 will be mapped to species 'Phytophthora nicotianae'
NCBI:txid4790 instead.

Will map anything else to the parent genus, although generally it will
be skipped via the reject_species_name(...) function, e.g.

* no-rank entry 'unclassified Pythium' NCBI:txid228096 would be mapped to
  Pythium NCBI:txid4797 - although we'd not interested in importing any
  unclassified entries.
* no-rank entry 'environmental samples' NCBI:txid660914 would be mapped to
  genus 'Hyaloperonospora' NCBI:txid184462 - but we skip this.
* entry 'uncultured Hyaloperonospora' NCBI:txid660915 would be mapped to
  genus 'Hyaloperonospora' NCBI:txid184462 - but we skip uncultured.

However, if you wanted to import this part of the tree:

* clade entry 'Skeletonema marinoi-dohrnii complex' NCBI:txid1171708 would
  be mapped to genus 'Skeletonema' NCBI:txid2842

Yields (genus taxid, node name) tuples.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.taxdump.main
documentation: Load an NCBI taxdump into a database.

modules.name: thapbi_pict
documentation:
THAPBI *Phytophthora* ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT).

You would typically use THAPBI PICT via the command line tool it defines:

```
$ thapbi_pict --help
...
```

However, it is also possible to call functions etc from within Python. The
top level package currently only defines the tool version:

> ```pycon
> >>> from thapbi_pict import __version__
> >>> print(__version__)
> 1.0...
> ```

The [tool documentation](https://thapbi-pict.readthedocs.io/) is
hosted by [Read The Docs](https://readthedocs.org/), generated
automatically from the `docs/` folder of the [software repository](https://github.com/peterjc/thapbi-pict/) and the *"docstrings"*
within the source code which document the Python API.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.utils
documentation: Helper functions for THAPB-PICT code.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.valid_marker_name
documentation:
Check the proposed string valid for use as a marker name.

Want to be able to use the string for file or directory names, and also
column names etc in reports. At very least should reject whitespace, line
breaks, and slashes.

Also rejecting all digits, as might want to accept integers as argument
(e.g. cluster array job mapping task numbers to marker numbers).

Also rejecting the underscore, as may want to use it as a field separator
in sequence names (e.g. `marker_md5_abundance`), and full stop as may
use it as a field separator in filenames.

May want to relax this later, thus defining this central function.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.primer_clean
documentation:
Handle non-IUPAC entries in primers, maps I for inosine to N.

```pycon
>>> primer_clean("I")
'N'
```

Inosine is found naturally at the wobble position of tRNA, and can match
any base. Structurally similar to guanine (G), it preferentially binds
cytosine (C). It sometimes used in primer design (Ben-Dov et al, 2006),
where degeneracy N would give similar results.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.reject_species_name
documentation:
Reject species names like 'environmental samples' or 'uncultured ...'.

Will also reject names with ";" in them as used in the classifier and
reports to combine multiple species entries.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.genus_species_name
documentation:
Return name, genus with species if present.

Copes with species being None (or empty string).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.genus_species_split
documentation:
Return (genus, species) splitting on first space.

If there are no spaces, returns (name, '') instead.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.species_level
documentation:
Is this prediction at species level.

Returns True for a binomial name (at least one space), False for genus
only or no prediction.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.onebp_substitutions
documentation:
Generate all 1bp substitutions of the sequence.

Assumes unambiguous IUPAC codes A, C, G, T only.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.onebp_deletions
documentation:
Generate all variants of sequence with 1bp deletion.

Assumes unambiguous IUPAC codes A, C, G, T only.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.onebp_inserts
documentation:
Generate all variants of sequence with 1bp insert.

Assumes unambiguous IUPAC codes A, C, G, T only.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.expand_IUPAC_ambiguity_codes
documentation:
Convert to upper case and iterate over possible unabmigous interpretations.

This is a crude recursive implementation, intended for use on sequences with
just a few ambiguity codes in them - it may not scale very well!

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.md5seq
documentation:
Return MD5 32-letter hex digest of the (upper case) sequence.

```pycon
>>> md5seq("ACGT")
'f1f8f4bf413b16ad135722aa4591043e'
```

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.md5_hexdigest
documentation: Return the MD5 hex-digest of the given file.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.cmd_as_string
documentation:
Express a list command as a suitably quoted string.

Intended for using in debugging or error messages.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.run
documentation:
Run a command via subprocess, abort if fails.

Returns a subprocess.CompletedProcess object, or None if all attempts fail.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.abundance_from_read_name
documentation:
Extract abundance from SWARM style read name.

```pycon
>>> abundance_from_read_name("9e8f051c64c2b9cc3b6fcb27559418ca_988")
988
```

If fails, will return one.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.split_read_name_abundance
documentation:
Split SWARM style read name into prefix and abundance.

```pycon
>>> split_read_name_abundance("9e8f051c64c2b9cc3b6fcb27559418ca_988")
('9e8f051c64c2b9cc3b6fcb27559418ca', 988)
```

If fails to detect the abundance, will return the original text
as the prefix with an abundance of 1.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.abundance_values_in_fasta
documentation: Return unique count, total abundance, and maximum abundances by spike-in.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.abundance_filter_fasta
documentation: Apply a minimum abundance filter to a FASTA file.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.file_to_sample_name
documentation:
Given filename (with or without a directory name), return sample name only.

i.e. XXX.fasta, XXX.fastq.gz, XXX.method.tsv --> XXX

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.find_requested_files
documentation:
Interpret a list of filenames and/or foldernames.

The extensions argument can be a tuple.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.find_paired_files
documentation:
Interpret a list of filenames and/or foldernames to find pairs.

Looks for paired files named XXX.ext1 and XXX.ext2 which can be
in different directories - duplicated filenames (in different
directories) are considered to be an error.

Having XXX.ext1 without XXX.ext2 is an error in strict mode, or a warning
in debug mode, otherwise silently ignored.

Having XXX.ext2 without XXX.ext1 is silently ignored.

The arguments ext1 and ext2 should include the leading dot.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.export_sample_biom
documentation:
Export a sequence vs samples counts BIOM table, with metadata.

Similar to the export_sample_tsv file (our TSV output), expects same
arguments as loaded from one of our TSV files via the parse_sample_tsv
function.

Will save a BIOM v2 HDF5 file if possible and return True. If output
fails (e.g. cannot import the biom Python library), returns False.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.export_sample_tsv
documentation:
Export a sequence vs sample counts TSV table, with metadata.

The TSV file ought to be readable by the parse_sample_tsv function, and
is first generated in our pipeline by the sample-tally command, and then
extended by the classify command to add taxonomic sequence metadata.

If the output tabular file argument is "-", it writes to stdout (not
supported with gzipped mode).

With no sequence metadata this should be accepted as a TSV BIOM file.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.parse_sample_tsv
documentation:
Parse file of sample abundances and sequence (etc).

Optional argument min_abundance is applied to the per sequence per sample
values (i.e. the matrix elements, not the row/column totals).

Columns are:
* Sequence label, <marker>/<identifier>_<abundance>
* Column per sample giving the sequence count
* Sequence itself
* Optional additional columns for sequence metadata (e.g. chimera flags)

Supports optional sample metadata header too as # prefixed header lines.

Returns dictionaries of:
* Sequence keyed on [<marker>, <identitifer>], string
* Sequence metadata keyed [<marker>, <identitifer>], dict of key:value pairs
* Sample metadata keyed on [<sample>], dict of key:value pairs
* Counts keyed on 3-tuple [<marker>, <identifier>, <sample>], integer

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.parse_species_tsv
documentation:
Parse file of species assignments/predictions by sequence.

Yields tuples of marker name (from the file header line), sequence name,
taxid, and genus_species.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.load_metadata
documentation:
Load specified metadata as several lists.

The encoding argument can be None or "", meaning use the default.

The columns argument should be a string like "1,3,5" - a comma
separated list of columns to output. The column numbers are assumed
to be one-based as provided by the command line user.

The name row indicates which row in the table contains the names
or descriptions of the metadata columns (one-based).

The index column is assumed to contain one or more sequenced sample
names separated by the character specified (default is semi-colon).
This one-to-many mapping reflecting that a single field sample could
be sequenced more than once (e.g. technical replicates). These sample
names are matched against the file name stems, see function find_metadata.

The metadata table rows are sorted based on the requested columns.

Return values:

- Dict mapping FASTQ stems to metadata tuples
- Ordered dict mapping metadata tuples to lists of FASTQ stems
- list of the N field names
- Color grouping offset into the N values

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.load_fasta_header
documentation: Parse our FASTA hash-comment line header as a dict.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.kmers
documentation: Make set of all kmers in the given sequence.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.utils.is_spike_in
documentation: Return spike-in name if sequence matches, else empty string.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm
documentation:
Object Relational Mapping for marker sequence database.

Using SQLalchemy, the Python classes defined here give us a
database schema and the code to import/export the data as
Python objects.
Copyright 2018-2023 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.connect_to_db
documentation:
Create engine and return session bound to it.

```pycon
>>> session = connect_to_db("sqlite:///:memory:", echo=True)
20...
```

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Base
documentation:
Base class for SQLAlchemy ORM declarations.

See the SQLAlchemy 2.0 documentation. This is expected to be
compatible with type checkers like mypy.

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.DataSource

methods.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.DataSource.__repr__
documentation: Represent a DataSource database entry as a string.

class_variables.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.DataSource.name
documentation: e.g. NCBI, Legacy v0.005

class_variables.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.DataSource.uri
documentation: e.g. traceable filename or URL

Database entry for a data source (NCBI, curated, etc).

Each accession is expected to be unique within a data source.

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Taxonomy

methods.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Taxonomy.__repr__
documentation: Represent a taxonomy database entry as a string.

class_variables.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Taxonomy.species
documentation: source may have variant/strain?
Database entry for a species' taxonomy entry.

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Synonym

methods.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Synonym.__repr__
documentation: Represent a synonym database entry as a string.

class_variables.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.Synonym.name
documentation: genus and species in one

Database entry for a synonym of a taxonomy entry.

In addition to direct synonyms, includes the names and synonyms of any
child nodes of the species (e.g. variants, strains, etc).

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.MarkerDef
documentation: Database entry for a marker listing primers and amplicon length limits.

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.MarkerSeq

methods.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.MarkerSeq.__repr__
documentation: Represent a marker database reference sequence as a string.
Database entry for a single marker reference sequence.

classes.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.SeqSource

class_variables.name: thapbi_pict.db_orm.SeqSource.source_accession
documentation: Hopefully unique within source_id
Database entry for source of a marker sequence entry.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.edit_graph
documentation:
Generate edit-distance network graph from FASTA files.

This implements the `thapbi_pict edit-graph ...` command.
Copyright 2019-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.edit_graph.write_pdf
documentation: Render NetworkX graph to PDF using GraphViz fdp.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.edit_graph.write_xgmml
documentation: Save graph in XGMML format suitable for Cytoscape import.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.edit_graph.main
documentation:
Run the edit-graph command with arguments from the command line.

This shows sequences from a database (possibly filtered with species/genus
limits) and/or selected sample-tally TSV file (optionally with classifier
output, and possibly with a minimum abundance limit set here).

Computes a Levenshtein edit-distance matrix from the selected sequences,
which can be exported as a matrix, but is usually converted into a graph
of unique sequences as nodes, with short edit distances as edges.

Graph node size is scaled by sample count (number of FASTA files that it
appears in), and colored by assigned species (from a classifier TSV file).

modules.name: thapbi_pict.db_import
documentation:
Shared code for THAPBI PICT to import FASTA into our database.

This code is used for importing NCBI formatted FASTA files, our curated ITS1
sequence FASTA file databases, and other other FASTA naming conventions.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.parse_ncbi_fasta_entry
documentation:
Split an entry of Accession Genus Species-name Description.

Returns a two-tuple: taxid (always zero), presumed genus-species (may be
the empty string).

```pycon
>>> parse_ncbi_fasta_entry("LC159493.1 Phytophthora drechsleri genes ...")
(0, 'Phytophthora drechsleri')
>>> parse_ncbi_fasta_entry("A57915.1 Sequence 20 from Patent EP0751227")
(0, '')
>>> parse_ncbi_fasta_entry("Y08654.1 P.cambivora ribosomal internal ...")
(0, '')
```

If a list of known species are used, then right most word is dropped until
the text matches a known name. This discards any description (and strain
level information if the list is only to species level).

If there is no match to the provided names, heuristics are used but this
defaults to the first two words.

Dividing the species name into genus, species, strain etc is not handled
here.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.parse_ncbi_taxid_entry
documentation:
Find any NCBI taxid as a pattern in the text.

Returns a two-tuple of taxid (zero if not found), and an
empty string (use the taxonomy table in the DB to get the
genus-species).

Uses a regular expression based on taxid=\<digits>, and
only considers the first match:

```pycon
>>> parse_ncbi_taxid_entry("HQ013219 Phytophthora arenaria [taxid=]")
(0, '')
>>> parse_ncbi_taxid_entry("HQ013219 Phytophthora arenaria [taxid=123] [taxid=456]")
(123, '')
```
TODO: Should we stop converting "Phytophthora humicola x Phytophthora inundata"
into "Phytophthora humicola x inundata"?

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.parse_curated_fasta_entry
documentation:
Split an entry of "Accession genus species etc" into fields.

Does not use the optional known_species argument.

Returns a two-tuple of taxid (0 unless taxid=... entry found), genus-species.

```pycon
>>> parse_curated_fasta_entry("HQ013219 Phytophthora arenaria")
(0, 'Phytophthora arenaria')
```

Will look for an NCBI taxid after the species name (and ignore anything
following that, such as other key=value entries):

```pycon
>>> parse_curated_fasta_entry("P13660 Phytophthora aff infestans taxid=907744 etc")
(907744, 'Phytophthora aff infestans')
```

In this example we expect the NCBI taxid will be matched to a pre-loaded
species name to be used in preference (i.e. 'Phytophthora aff. infestans'
with a dot in it).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.parse_sintax_fasta_entry
documentation:
Extract the species from SINTAX taxonomy annotation.

See <https://drive5.com/usearch/manual/tax_annot.html> which defines
this taxonomy annotation convention as used in USEARCH and VSEARCH.
The tax=names field is separated from other fields in the FASTA
description line by semi-colons, for example:

```pycon
>>> entry = "X80725_S000004313;tax=d:...,g:Escherichia/Shigella,s:Escherichia_coli"
>>> parse_sintax_fasta_entry(entry)
(0, 'Escherichia coli')
```

If there is no species entry (prefix `s:`) then the genus is returned
(prefix `g:`), else the empty string:

```pycon
>>> parse_sintax_fasta_entry("AB008314;tax=d:...,g:Streptococcus;")
(0, 'Streptococcus')
```

If the species entry is missing the genus information (which may happen
depending how the file was generated), that is inferred heuristically:

```pycon
>>> entry = "X80725_S000004313;tax=d:...,g:Escherichia,s:coli"
>>> parse_sintax_fasta_entry(entry)
(0, 'Escherichia coli')
```

This can be unclear:

```pycon
>>> entry = ">X80725_S000004313;tax=d:...,g:Escherichia/Shigella,s:Escherichia_coli"
>>> parse_sintax_fasta_entry(entry)
(0, 'Escherichia coli')
```

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.parse_obitools_fasta_entry
documentation:
Parse species from the OBITools extended FASTA header.

See <https://pythonhosted.org/OBITools/attributes.html> which explains that
OBITools splits the FASTA line into identifier, zero or more key=value;
entries, and a free text description.

We are specifically interested in the species_name, genus_name (used if
species_name is missing), and taxid.

```pycon
>>> entry = "AP009202 species_name=Abalistes stellaris; taxid=392897; ..."
>>> parse_obitools_fasta_entry(entry)
(392897, 'Abalistes stellaris')
```

Note this will *not* try to parse any key=value entries embedded in the
first word (which taken as the identifier).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.load_taxonomy
documentation: Pre-load all the species and synonym names as a set.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.lookup_species
documentation: Find this species entry in the taxonomy/synonym table (if present).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.lookup_genus
documentation: Find genus entry via taxonomy/synonym table (if present).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.import_fasta_file
documentation: Import a FASTA file into the database.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.db_import.main
documentation:
Import FASTA file(s) into the database.

For curated FASTA files, use convention "simple" (default here and at the
command line), and specify any multi-entry separator you are using.

For NCBI files, convention "ncbi" and for the separator use Ctrl+A (type
`-s $'\001'` at the command line) if appropriate, or "" or None
(function default) if single entries are expected.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.conflicts
documentation: Explore conflicts at species and genus level.
Copyright 2019-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.conflicts.main
documentation:
Implement the `thapbi_pict conflicts` subcommand.

Looks for taxonomy conflicts at marker, genus or species level, with the
number of marker or genus level conflicts used as the return code. i.e.
Unix failure (non-zero) when there are marker or genus level conflicts.

A marker level conflict is when a unique sequence appears in the DB under
more than one marker name (e.g. both COI and ITS1), which is most likely
an error in the DB construction.

Genus level conflicts are where a unique sequence in the DB is reported
from more than one genus, which is considered undesirable. Similarly for
species level conflicts, but for some markers this is sadly common and not
considered to be an error.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.sample_tally
documentation:
Prepare a non-redundant TSV file using MD5 naming.

This implements the `thapbi_pict sample-tally ...` command.
Copyright 2022-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.sample_tally.main
documentation:
Implement the `thapbi_pict sample-tally` command.

Arguments min_length and max_length are applied while loading the input
per-sample FASTA files.

Argument algorithm is a string, "-" for no read correction (denoising),
"unoise-l" for our reimplementation of the UNOISE2 algorithm, or "usearch"
or "vsearch" to invoke those tools at the command line.

Arguments min_abundance and min_abundance_fraction are applied per-sample
(after denoising if being used), increased by pool if negative or
synthetic controls are given respectively. Comma separated string argument
spike_genus is treated case insensitively.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.dump
documentation:
Dumping out marker database to text files.

This implements the `thapbi_pict dump ...` command.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.dump.none_str
documentation: Turn value into a string, special case None to empty string.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.dump.main
documentation: Run the database dump with arguments from the command line.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.prepare
documentation:
Prepare raw amplicon sequencing reads (trimming, merging, etc).

This implements the `thapbi_pict prepare-reads ...` command.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.find_fastq_pairs
documentation:
Interpret a list of filenames and/or foldernames.

Returns a list of tuples (stem, left filename, right filename)
where stem is intended for use in logging and output naming,
and may include a directory name.

The filenames will be normalised relative to the current directory
(so that we can directly compare file lists which could have been
defined inconsistently by the user).

Will ignore "-" if present in the inputs.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.parse_cutadapt_stdout
documentation:
Extract FASTA count before and after cutadapt.

```pycon
>>> parse_cutadapt_stdout(
...     "...\n"
...     "Total reads processed: 5,869\n"
...     "...\n"
...     "Reads written (passing filters): 5,861 (99.9%)\n"
...     "..."
... )
(5869, 5861)
```

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.run_cutadapt
documentation:
Run cutadapt on a single file (i.e. after merging paired FASTQ).

The input and/or output files may be compressed as long as they
have an appropriate suffix (e.g. gzipped with `.gz` suffix).

Returns FASTA count before and after cutadapt.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.parse_flash_stdout
documentation:
Extract FASTQ pair count before/after running flash.

```pycon
>>> parse_flash_stdout(
...     "...\n"
...     "[FLASH] Read combination statistics:\n"
...     "[FLASH]     Total pairs:      6105\n"
...     "[FLASH]     Combined pairs:   5869\n"
...     "..."
... )
(6105, 5869)
```

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.run_flash
documentation:
Run FLASH on a pair of trimmed FASTQ files to merge overlapping pairs.

Returns two integers, FASTQ pair count for input and output files.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.save_nr_fasta
documentation:
Save a dictionary of sequences and counts as a FASTA file.

Writes a FASTA file with header lines starting # (which not all tools will
accept as valid FASTA format).

The output FASTA records are named `>MD5_abundance\n`, which is the
default style used in SWARM. This could in future be generalised,
for example `>MD5;size=abundance;\n` for the VSEARCH default.

Results are sorted by decreasing abundance then alphabetically by
sequence.

Returns the total and number of unique sequences accepted (above any
minimum abundance specified).

Use output_fasta='-' for standard out.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.make_nr_fasta
documentation:
Trim and make non-redundant FASTA/Q file from FASTA input.

Makes a non-redundant FASTA file with the sequences named
`>MD5_abundance\n`.

For FASTQ files all input reads are treated as abundance one
(using weighted_input=True gives an error).

If FASTA input and weighted_input=True, reads must follow
`>identifier_abundance\n` naming and the abundance is used.
Otherwise all treated as abundance one.

Makes a non-redundant FASTA file with the sequences named
`>MD5_abundance\n`.

Returns the total number of accepted reads before de-duplication
(integer), number of those unique (integer), and the total number
of those which passed the minimum abundance threshold (integer),
and number of those which are unique (integer).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.merge_paired_reads
documentation: Create NR FASTA file by overlap merging the paired FASTQ files.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.prepare_sample
documentation:
Create marker-specific FASTA file for sample from paired FASTQ.

Applies abundance threshold, and min/max length.

Returns pre-threshold total read count, accepted unique sequence count,
accepted total read count, and the absolute abundance threshold used
(higher of the given absolute threshold or the given fractional threshold).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.marker_cut
documentation: Apply primer-trimming for given markers.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.load_marker_defs
documentation: Load marker definitions and any spike-in sequences from the DB.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.prepare.main
documentation:
Implement the `thapbi_pict prepare-reads` command.

For use in the pipeline command, returns a filename listing of the FASTA
files created.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.ena_submit
documentation:
Code for sample submission to ENA/SRA.

This implements the `thapbi_pict ena-submit ...` command.
Copyright 2019-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.ena_submit.load_md5
documentation: Return a dict mapping given filenames to MD5 digests.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.ena_submit.write_table
documentation:
Write read file table for ENA upload.

Leave library name as "-" and the folder name will be used.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.ena_submit.main
documentation: Implement the `thapbi_pict ena-submit` command.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.assess
documentation:
Assess classification of marker reads at species level.

This implements the `thapbi_pict assess ...` command.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.load_tsv
documentation: Update dict mapping of (marker, MD5) to semi-colon separated species string.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.sp_for_sample
documentation: Return semi-colon separated species string from FASTA files via dict.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.sp_in_tsv
documentation:
Return semi-colon separated list of species in column 2.

Will ignore genus level predictions.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.tally_files
documentation:
Make dictionary tally confusion matrix of species assignments.

Rather than the values simply being an integer count, they are
the set of MD5 identifiers (take the length for the count).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.class_list_from_tally_and_db_list
documentation: Sorted list of all class names used in a confusion table dict.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.save_mapping
documentation: Output tally table of expected species to predicted sp.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.save_confusion_matrix
documentation: Output a multi-class confusion matrix as a tab-separated table.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.extract_binary_tally
documentation:
Extract single-class TP, FP, FN, TN from multi-class confusion tally.

Reduces the mutli-class expectation/prediction to binary - did they
include the class of interest, or not?

Returns a 4-tuple of values, True Positives (TP), False Positives (FP),
False Negatives (FN), True Negatives (TN), which sum to the tally total.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.extract_global_tally
documentation:
Process multi-label confusion matrix (tally dict) to TP, FP, FN, TN.

If the input data has no negative controls, all there will be no
true negatives (TN).

Returns a 4-tuple of values, True Positives (TP), False Positives (FP),
False Negatives (FN), True Negatives (TN).

These values are analogous to the classical binary classifier approach,
but are NOT the same. Even if applied to single class expected and
predicted values, results differ:

- Expect none, predict none - 1xTN
- Expect none, predict A - 1xFP
- Expect A, predict none - 1xFN
- Expect A, predict A - 1xTP
- Expect A, predict B - 1xFP (the B), 1xFN (missing A)
- Expect A, predict A&B - 1xTP (the A), 1xFP (the B)
- Expect A&B, predict A&B - 2xTP
- Expect A&B, predict A - 1xTP, 1xFN (missing B)
- Expect A&B, predict A&C - 1xTP (the A), 1xFP (the C), 1xFN (missing B)

The TP, FP, FN, TN sum will exceed the tally total.  For each tally
entry, rather than one of TP, FP, FN, TN being incremented (weighted
by the tally count), several can be increased.

If the input data has no negative controls, all there will be no TN.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.assess.main
documentation:
Implement the (sample/species level) `thapbi_pict assess` command.

The inputs argument is a list of filenames and/or folders.

Must provide:
\* at least one XXX.\<method>.tsv file
\* at least one XXX.\<known>.tsv file

These files can cover multiple samples as the sample-tally based classifier
output, or legacy per-sample \<sample>.\<known>.tsv files.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.versions
documentation:
Helper code to get command line tool versions.

Defines various functions to check a tool is on the `$PATH` and if so,
return the tool version as a short string (sometimes including a date).

These functions are called from various THAPBI-PICT subcommands which call
external tools to ensure a clear missing dependency message, and to log the
version of the external tool used.

If the tool is not on the path, the commands all return None.

If we cannot parse the output, again the commands return None - which is
likely an indication of a major version change, meaning the tool ought to be
re-evaluated for use with THAPBI-PICT.
Copyright 2019-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.check_rapidfuzz
documentation: Check can import rapidfuzz and confirm recent enough.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.check_tools
documentation:
Verify the named tools are present, log versions if debug=True.

Argument names should be an iterable of tool binary names.

If all the tools are present, returns a list of version strings.

If any tools are missing (or have a version we could not parse),
aborts.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.version_graphviz_fdp
documentation:
Return the version of the GraphViz tool fdp (as a short string).

Depends on the -V switch:

```
$ fdp -V
fdp - graphviz version 9.0.0 (0)
```

In the above example, it would behave as follows:

```pycon
>>> version_graphviz_fdp()
'9.0.0'
```

If the command is not on the path, returns None.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.version_blast
documentation:
Return the version of the NCBI BLAST+ suite's blastn (as a short string).

In the absence of a built in version switch like `-v`, this works by
parsing the short help output with `-h` (which does vary between the
tools in the suite):

```
$ makeblastdb -h | grep BLAST
Application to create BLAST databases, version 2.16.0+

$ blastn -h | grep BLAST
Nucleotide-Nucleotide BLAST 2.16.0+
```

In the above examples, it would behave as follows:

```pycon
>>> version_blast("makeblastdb")
'2.16.0+'
>>> version_blast("blastn")
'2.16.0+'
```

If the command is not on the path, returns None.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.version_cutadapt
documentation:
Return the version of cutadapt (as a short string).

Uses the output with `--version`:

```
$ cutadapt --version
4.7
```

It would capture this:

```pycon
>>> version_cutadapt()
'4.7'
```

If the command is not on the path, returns None.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.version_flash
documentation:
Return the version of flash (as a short string).

Parses the output with `-v`:

```
$ flash -v | head -n 1
FLASH v1.2.11
```

It would capture the version from the first line as follows:

```pycon
>>> version_flash()
'1.2.11'
```

If the command is not on the path, returns None.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.version_usearch
documentation:
Return the version of usearch (as a short string).

Uses the output with `--version`:

```
$ usearch --version
usearch v11.0.667_i86linux32
```

It would capture this:

```pycon
>>> version_usearch()  # doctest: +SKIP
'11.0.667'
```

If the command is not on the path, returns None.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.versions.version_vsearch
documentation:
Return the version of vsearch (as a short string).

Uses the output with `--version`:

```
$ vsearch --version
vsearch v2.28.1_macos_x86_64, 8.0GB RAM, 8 cores
...
```

It would capture this:

```pycon
>>> version_vsearch()
'2.28.1'
```

If the command is not on the path, returns None.

modules.name: thapbi_pict.summary
documentation:
Summarise classification results at sample and read level.

This implements the `thapbi_pict summary ...` command.

The code uses the term metadata to refer to the user-provided
information about each sample (via a plain text TSV table),
and statistics for the internally tracked information about
each sample like the number of raw reads in the original FASTQ
files (via header lines in the intermediate FASTA files).
Copyright 2019-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.summary._sp_display
documentation: Format species classification for reports, see also _sp_sort_key.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.summary._sp_sort_key
documentation:
Sort unknowns after knowns, see also _sp_display.

Want this order:

  * Genus1 species1
  * Genus1 species2
  * Genus1 (unknown species) -- aka "Genus1"
  * Genus2 species1
  * ...
  * GenusN species1
  * ...
  * GenusN (unknown species) -- aka "GenusN"
  * Unknown - aka ""

See also function _sp_display which handles the genus only and unknown.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.summary.color_bands
documentation: Return a list for formats, one for each sample.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.summary.sample_summary
documentation:
Create samples (rows) vs species (cols) report.

The expectation is that the inputs represent all the samples from
a meaningful group, likely from multiple sequencing runs (plates).

functions.name: thapbi_pict.summary.read_summary
documentation:
Create reads (rows) vs species (cols) report.

The expectation is that the inputs represent all the samples
from one (96 well) plate, or some other meaningful batch.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.summary.main
documentation:
Implement the `thapbi_pict summary` command.

The expectation is that the inputs represent all the samples from
a meaningful group, likely from multiple sequencing runs (plates).

modules.name: thapbi_pict.__main__
documentation:
Defines the `thapbi_pict` command line tool.

This works via `setup.py` where under `entry_points` we define a
`console_scripts` entry for `thapbi_pict` (executable name) pointing to
the `main()` function defined in this Python file.
Copyright 2018-2024 by Peter Cock, The James Hutton Institute.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock.
Revisions copyright 2024 by Peter Cock, University of Strathclyde.
All rights reserved.
This file is part of the THAPBI Phytophthora ITS1 Classifier Tool (PICT),
and is released under the "MIT License Agreement". Please see the LICENSE
file that should have been included as part of this package.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.check_cpu
documentation: If zero use all available CPUs, otherwise cap the given value.
Argument validation functions

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.check_input_file

Command line validation of output stem value.

Returns the output directory, or aborts.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.check_output_directory
documentation: Command line validation of output directory value.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.expand_database_argument
documentation: Expand an SQLite3 filename to an SQLalchemy URL.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.validate_markers
documentation: Confirm requests are a subset of those defined.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.load_tax

Subcommand dispatch

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.db_import
documentation: Subcommand to import a FASTA file into a basebase.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.dump
documentation: Subcommand to dump a database to a text file.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.conflicts
documentation: Subcommand to look for taxonomic conflicts in a database.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.prepare_reads
documentation: Subcommand to prepare FASTA files from paired FASTQ reads.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.fasta_nr
documentation: Subcommand to prepare non-redundant MD5 named FASTA files.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.denoise
documentation: Subcommand to denoise FASTA file(s) using UNOISE read-correction.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.sample_tally
documentation: Subcommand to tally per-sample FASTA files using MD5 naming.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.classify
documentation: Subcommand to classify FASTA sequences using a database.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.assess_classification
documentation: Subcommand to assess classification using known true taxonomy.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.summary
documentation: Subcommand to run sample and read summary reports.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.edit_graph
documentation: Subcommand to create sequence-level edit-distance graph.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.pipeline
documentation: Subcommand to run the default classification pipeline.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.ena_submit
documentation: Subcommand to run multiple-output-folder summary at sample level.

functions.name: thapbi_pict.__main__.main
documentation:
Execute the command line script thapbi_pict.

Note this needs to cope with no command line information via the Python
function arguments because that is how it is invoked by `thapbi_pict`
via setuptools.

To facilitate automated testing etc, it can also be called with an
argument list.
Command line definition
